role_summary: Co-founder of the Seattle chapter of the Black Panther Party with his brother Aaron Dixon (1968), the first chapter formed outside California.
biographical_paragraph: Elmer Dixon helped establish the Seattle chapter of the Black Panther Party in 1968 alongside his brother Aaron Dixon, the chapter being the first formed outside California [src: 2]. In April 1968, the week of Martin Luther King Jr.'s assassination, the brothers attended the West Coast Black Student Union conference in San Francisco and were at Bobby Hutton's funeral; in the days surrounding those events they met with Black Panther leadership including Bobby Seale and Kathleen Cleaver, beginning the conversation that produced the Seattle chapter [src: 2]. His brother Aaron is interviewee entry 1 in this archive; their joint founding of the Seattle chapter is also the seed for Aaron's 2012 memoir My People Are Rising [src: 2]. His Library of Congress oral history is entry 37 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1].

sources.title: Elmer Dixon oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
